visa_types.name: Eu Relatives
Medical Treatment
visa_types.documents_required:
Requisite documents for medical visa (short term visa up to 90 days) 
Click here for Medical Checklist
Kindly note that the person accompanying the patient will have to provide documents as per the Tourist visa.
Important note:
- Travel Insurance must have at least 2 days additional after the departure date from the Schengen states and should be issued by a local insurance company.
- Applicant with a valid QID can submit the visa request only on the passport that is registered on the back of the QID, passport number on the QID and physical passport must match

visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for visa applications can take minimum 15 calendar days. 
The processing time can further increase to 45 calendar days. Reasons why your visa application may take longer include: 
- Your application is impacted by high volume. 
- Your application requires further consideration. 
- You need to provide additional documents.
Some Schengen member states want to exchange information about who is applying for Schengen visas. This check (known as ‘consultation’) is mandatory for certain nationalities and can take up to 7 days. This period falls within the 45-day processing time.
Possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/e6139169-6ec7-4374-a50a-eb2333411e3f_en (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/iT1kCYMLMhLw9rnmFwIRUx_8zW?domain=home-affairs.ec.europa.eu)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the
                                                                        interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy,the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview
                                                                        ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from
                                                                        the Visa Application Centre. 
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Embassy of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application
                                                                        is given - summer period being the slowest.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the
                                                                        interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy,the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview
                                                                        ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from
                                                                        the Visa Application Centre. 
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Embassy of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application
                                                                        is given - summer period being the slowest.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## Visa Fees

[ita > qat > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
Exceptions and  Exemption of visa fee

[ita > qat > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 2]
Service fees:

[ita > qat > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 3]

## Documents Required
Requisite documents for medical visa (short term visa up to 90 days) 
Click here for Medical Checklist
Kindly note that the person accompanying the patient will have to provide documents as per the Tourist visa.
Important note:
- Travel Insurance must have at least 2 days additional after the departure date from the Schengen states and should be issued by a local insurance company.
- Applicant with a valid QID can submit the visa request only on the passport that is registered on the back of the QID, passport number on the QID and physical passport must match

## Photo Specifications
 Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General 
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use white colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.
- VFS staff will verify applicant identity at the biometric booth, applicant appearance in person and the photo on the passport must match and applicant need to be identifiable from their official documents or the application cannot be accepted
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 x 4.5 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
                                                                            
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application
                                                                        form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6cwki7jx1ZTIFwAKGgS8Zh/fc3c4177b9c92c6f14805d47c686f0ed/Annex-A-Photograph-Guidelines.pdf) for Photo Specifications guidelines.

## Processing Time
The processing time for visa applications can take minimum 15 calendar days. 
The processing time can further increase to 45 calendar days. Reasons why your visa application may take longer include: 
- Your application is impacted by high volume. 
- Your application requires further consideration. 
- You need to provide additional documents.
Some Schengen member states want to exchange information about who is applying for Schengen visas. This check (known as ‘consultation’) is mandatory for certain nationalities and can take up to 7 days. This period falls within the 45-day processing time.
Possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/e6139169-6ec7-4374-a50a-eb2333411e3f_en (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/iT1kCYMLMhLw9rnmFwIRUx_8zW?domain=home-affairs.ec.europa.eu)

## Download Forms
 Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/sq9uy13TCNOkeehgMZRsl/a7f2a8efcd29e392072dc861573a75f4/formulario-application-form-english.pdf)

For application submitted on Thursday the processing time is counted from the the next working day after the submission
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy, the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview (if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy, the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview (if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.

## Visa Fees

[ita > qat > en > Table:Elective visa > Visa Fees]
Service fees:

[ita > qat > en > Table:Elective visa > Visa Fees]
Please note: 
Additional services 
- The applicable visa fee in Qatari Riyals is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- For long term visa (National Visa) Applicant shall be levied a service charge of QAR 30 per application over and above visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
Click here for Elective visa Checklist

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use white colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.
- VFS staff will verify applicant identity at the biometric booth, applicant appearance in person and the photo on the passport must match and applicant need to be identifiable from their official documents or the application cannot be accepted
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 x 4.5 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels
                                                                                one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert GroupFormat). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be
                                                                            glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6cwki7jx1ZTIFwAKGgS8Zh/fc3c4177b9c92c6f14805d47c686f0ed/Annex-A-Photograph-Guidelines.pdf) for Photo Specifications guidelines.

## Processing Time
The processing time for visa applications is approximately to 20 calendar days depending in the Embassy decision.
For application submitted on Thursday the processing time is counted from the the next working day after the submission

## Download Forms
Application Form - Long Stay

visa_types.forms: Application Form for STUDY LONG STAY
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about the Schengen area.
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy,the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Individuals travelling to Italy with the purpose of Study can apply for short or long term visas, depending on the duration of their stay (less or more than 90 days).
Please Note:
- An updated Study visa check list is available. Please read carefully all the important information and the required mandatory documentation to submit when applying
- A new form called AFFIDAVIT OF SUPPORT FOR STUDY must be submitted if the applicant is not financially independent. Please refer to the check list for the details
- Walk in appointment for study purpose will be available on MONDAYS, if the student cannot secure an appointment on line. Only Mondays walk in appointment for study purpose are free of charge.
- Students enrolling to a university course (Laurea, Laurea Magistrale, Diploma accademico di primo livello e Diploma Accademico di secondo livello, corsi accademici AFAM) must apply for a visa by November 30th 2026, even if they are attending classes online
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about the Schengen area.
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy,the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Individuals travelling to Italy with the purpose of Study can apply for short or long term visas, depending on the duration of their stay (less or more than 90 days).
Please Note:
- An updated Study visa check list is available. Please read carefully all the important information and the required mandatory documentation to submit when applying
- A new form called AFFIDAVIT OF SUPPORT FOR STUDY must be submitted if the applicant is not financially independent. Please refer to the check list for the details
- Walk in appointment for study purpose will be available on MONDAYS, if the student cannot secure an appointment on line. Only Mondays walk in appointment for study purpose are free of charge.
- Students enrolling to a university course (Laurea, Laurea Magistrale, Diploma accademico di primo livello e Diploma Accademico di secondo livello, corsi accademici AFAM) must apply for a visa by November 30th 2026, even if they are attending classes online

## Visa Fees

[ita > qat > en > Table:Study > Visa Fees 2]
Please note: 
Additional services 
- The applicable visa fee in Qatari Riyals is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- For long term visa (National Visa) Applicant shall be levied a service charge of QAR 30 per application over and above visa fees.
- All fees are non-refundable

## Documents Required
## STUDY (National Visa)

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use white colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.
- VFS staff will verify applicant identity at the biometric booth, applicant appearance in person and the photo on the passport must match and applicant need to be identifiable from their official documents or the application cannot be accepted
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 x 4.5 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels
                                                                                one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert GroupFormat). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be
                                                                            glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6cwki7jx1ZTIFwAKGgS8Zh/fc3c4177b9c92c6f14805d47c686f0ed/Annex-A-Photograph-Guidelines.pdf) for Photo Specifications guidelines.

## Processing Time
The processing time for visa applications can take minimum 15 calendar days.
The processing time can further increase to 45 calendar days. Reasons why your visa application may take longer include:
- Your application is impacted by high volume.
- Your application requires further consideration.
- You need to provide additional documents.
Some Schengen member states want to exchange information about who is applying for Schengen visas. This check (known as ‘consultation’) is mandatory for certain nationalities and can take up to 7 days. This period falls within the 45-day processing time.
Possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/e6139169-6ec7-4374-a50a-eb2333411e3f_en (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/iT1kCYMLMhLw9rnmFwIRUx_8zW?domain=home-affairs.ec.europa.eu)

visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
- Click here for Tourist Checklist
- Click here for Tourist Checklist for Qatari Citizens
- Consent letter for QATARI Minors travelling with ONE parent
- Guarantor Letter
- Data Protection (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/64Qu6J8Xpg884IcoVDwwbF/ed14c265e243e40b500c5d8ed8978251/GDPR-Protection-of-Personale-Data-2022.pdf)
- Parental Authorization letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1reQJayxyE32enalH65SNY/a852ea3e3f09cbb64b86029d977809e8/Parental_Authorization_for_minors_travelling_without_the_parents_2026.pdf)
- Letter of consent
- Invitation letter (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1npCXAvvDrKY9eKf3OqdQw/6b267f92e007f87483defc85c7f46f12/invitation-letter-sep-2021.pdf)
Important note:
- Travel Insurance must have at least 2 days additional after the departure date from the Schengen states and should be issued by a local insurance company.
- For housemaid applicant employment contract must be made directly with the employer, have completed 1 year of employment and contract must stamped by the ministry of labour, for Pilipino nationals the employment contract must be stamped by the ministry of labour and the Philippine Embassy
- Applicant with a valid QID can submit the visa request only on the passport that is registered on the back of the QID, passport number on the QID and physical passport must match
All documents required from the checklist must be submitted in original for each application, if one family member is covering the expense his original employment letter and original bank statement should be submitted with his application and copy with the other family member.

- For housemaid applicant employment contract must be made directly with the employer, have completed 1 year of employment and contract must stamped by the ministry of labour, for Pilipino nationals the employment contract must be stamped by the ministry of labour and the Philippine Embassy
- Applicant with a valid QID can submit the visa request only on the passport that is registered on the back of the QID, passport number on the QID and physical passport must match
All documents required from the checklist must be submitted in original for each application, if one family member is covering the expense his original employment letter and original bank statement should be submitted with his application and copy with the other family member.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
 General 
 The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use white colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.
- VFS staff will verify applicant identity at the biometric booth, applicant appearance in person and the photo on the passport must match and applicant need to be identifiable from their official documents or the application cannot be accepted
Photo Size 
 The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 x 4.5 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
                                                                            
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6cwki7jx1ZTIFwAKGgS8Zh/fc3c4177b9c92c6f14805d47c686f0ed/Annex-A-Photograph-Guidelines.pdf) for Photo Specifications guidelines.

## Processing Time
The processing time for visa applications can take minimum 15 calendar days. 
The processing time can further increase to 45 calendar days. Reasons why your visa application may take longer include: 
- Your application is impacted by high volume. 
- Your application requires further consideration. 
- You need to provide additional documents.
Some Schengen member states want to exchange information about who is applying for Schengen visas. This check (known as ‘consultation’) is mandatory for certain nationalities and can take up to 7 days. This period falls within the 45-day processing time.
Possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/document/download/e6139169-6ec7-4374-a50a-eb2333411e3f_en (https://url.uk.m.mimecastprotect.com/s/iT1kCYMLMhLw9rnmFwIRUx_8zW?domain=home-affairs.ec.europa.eu)

## Download Forms
 Application Form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/sq9uy13TCNOkeehgMZRsl/a7f2a8efcd29e392072dc861573a75f4/formulario-application-form-english.pdf)

visa_types.name: National Visa Category D
visa_types.overview:
National Visa Category D
Long Term Visas (Category D) for Italy are issued by the Embassy of Italy in Qatar, applications can be submitted only through prior appointments.
The Italy Embassy is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Italy. You will be informed automatically when the residence permit has been authorized.
Your visa can only be issued to you in the Qatar. It cannot be forwarded to Italy and it cannot be issued in Italy after your arrival. After your arrival in Italy you must register as soon as possible with the Foreign Nationals Authority of your place of residence
visa_types.full_text:
## National Visa Category D
Long Term Visas (Category D) for Italy are issued by the Embassy of Italy in Qatar, applications can be submitted only through prior appointments.
The Italy Embassy is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Italy. You will be informed automatically when the residence permit has been authorized.
Your visa can only be issued to you in the Qatar. It cannot be forwarded to Italy and it cannot be issued in Italy after your arrival. After your arrival in Italy you must register as soon as possible with the Foreign Nationals Authority of your place of residence

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa, Category C
visa_types.overview:
The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination (determined by the purpose of the journey and the length of stay).
If your main destination cannot be ascertained, the Schengen state through which you first enter the territory of these countries is responsible for granting the visa.

Schengen Visa and Visa Code
In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission decides on the visa application at its own discretion, considering all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Qatar to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 days. Applicants are therefore requested to organize themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
visa_types.full_text:
The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination (determined by the purpose of the journey and the length of stay).
If your main destination cannot be ascertained, the Schengen state through which you first enter the territory of these countries is responsible for granting the visa.

Schengen Visa and Visa Code
In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission decides on the visa application at its own discretion, considering all the circumstances in any given individual case.
All visa applicants are advised to schedule an appointment and visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Qatar to submit their applications, together with all necessary documents
Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 days. Applicants are therefore requested to organize themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.

Overview

                                                                            from the Visa Application Centre.

                                                                            Interview (if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use white colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.
- VFS staff will verify applicant identity at the biometric booth, applicant appearance in person and the photo on the passport must match and applicant need to be identifiable from their official documents or the application cannot be accepted
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 x 4.5 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels
                                                                                one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert GroupFormat). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be
                                                                            glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6cwki7jx1ZTIFwAKGgS8Zh/fc3c4177b9c92c6f14805d47c686f0ed/Annex-A-Photograph-Guidelines.pdf) for Photo Specifications guidelines.

## Processing Time
The processing time for visa applications can take minimum 15 calendar days.
The processing time can further increase to 45 calendar days. Reasons why your visa application may take longer include:
- Your application is impacted by high volume.
- Your application requires further consideration.
- You need to provide additional documents.
Some Schengen member states want to exchange information about who is applying for Schengen visas. This check (known as ‘consultation’) is mandatory for certain nationalities and can take up to 7 days. This period falls within the 45-day processing time.
Possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the
                                                                        interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy, the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview
                                                                        ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from
                                                                        the Visa Application Centre. 
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Embassy of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application
                                                                        is given - summer period being the slowest.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the
                                                                        interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy, the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview
                                                                        ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from
                                                                        the Visa Application Centre. 
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Embassy of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant and the period of the year in which the application
                                                                        is given - summer period being the slowest.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the
                                                                        interactive map (http://ec.europa.eu/dgs/home-affairs/what-we-do/policies/borders-and-visas/visa-policy/map_en.htm) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Italy,the next working day and the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal Interview
                                                                        ( if applicable) with the Embassy of Italy . Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy of Italy, the decision together with the passport will be available for collection from
                                                                        the Visa Application Centre. 
Individuals travelling to Italy with the purpose of Study can apply for short- or long-term visas, depending on the duration of their stay (less or more than 90 days).
Please Note:
- An updated Study visa check list is available. Please read carefully all the important information and  the required mandatory documentation to submit when applying
- Walk in appointment for study purpose will be available on MONDAYS, if the student cannot secure an appointment on line. Only Mondays walk in appointment for study purpose are free of charge

                                                                        The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 x 4.5 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert GroupFormat). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
                                                                            
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
Click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6cwki7jx1ZTIFwAKGgS8Zh/fc3c4177b9c92c6f14805d47c686f0ed/Annex-A-Photograph-Guidelines.pdf) for Photo Specifications guidelines.

## Processing Time
The processing time for visa applications can take minimum 15 calendar days. 
The processing time can further increase to 45 calendar days. Reasons why your visa application may take longer include: 
- Your application is impacted by high volume. 
- Your application requires further consideration. 
- You need to provide additional documents.
Some Schengen member states want to exchange information about who is applying for Schengen visas. This check (known as ‘consultation’) is mandatory for certain nationalities and can take up to 7 days. This period falls within the 45-day processing time.
Possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal right to a Schengen visa. The ultimate decision regarding admission to the Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
